title: podman does not enable to update
body:
### Issue Description

On Linux-Mint 22.2 Is version 4.9.3. If I want to update with the command podman pull, the message "...already exists" comes up.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

On Linux-Mint 22.2 Is version 4.9.3. If I want to update with the command podman pull, the message "...already exists" comes up.


### Describe the results you received

On Linux-Mint 22.2 Is version 4.9.3. If I want to update with the command podman pull, the message "...already exists" comes up.

### Describe the results you expected

On Linux-Mint 22.2 Is version 4.9.3. If I want to update with the command podman pull, the message "...already exists" comes up.

### Additional information

comments.body:
Hi @HKaufmann2 

Can you please post a full reproducer? From the minimal information you have provided it's difficult to understand exactly what issue you're reporting.

Can you tell me what information you need. My English is not very good.

The problem is that I can't update Podman. You currently have version 5.7.1. With me is version 4.9.3.

Können Sie mir sagen welche Information Sie brauchen. Mein Englisch ist nicht besonders gut.

Das Problem ist, das ich Podman nicht aktualisieren kann. Sie haben aktuell die Version 5.7.1. Bei mir ist Version 4.9.3.

The upstream project is not responsible for what podman version are shipped as part of your distribution. You seems to be using an older version (Mint 22.2) so an old version is to be expected. If you want something newer you need to update the OS or install podman from somewhere else. We do not maintain our own repos, see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25219

Thank you for your answer. How can I set the current stable version to be installed in the compose file?

Danke für die Antwort. Wie kann ich in der compose Datei das so einstellen, dass die aktuelle stabile Version installiert wird?
